label: LLM Router
description: LLM-as-condition. Given a prompt + a configured set of named routes (each with a description), the model picks exactly one route by name. The chosen route is used as the outgoing `sourceHandle` so downstream branches gate naturally. Goes through the SAME /v1/chat/completions endpoint as `llm_completion` — model selection follows the platform's Smart Router + the DB-backed provider/model registry (SoT). Never bypasses into chatmode's V3 pipeline.

settings.name: prompt
settings.label: Prompt
settings.description: The classification question. Template-interpolated against the input.

settings.name: routes
settings.label: Routes
settings.description: Array of `{ name, description }` — the route names become outgoing `sourceHandle` values on edges. Description is what the model uses to decide.

settings.name: fallbackRoute
settings.label: Fallback route
settings.description: Route name to use when the model picks an invalid value. When unset, an invalid model pick fails the node.

settings.name: systemPrompt
settings.label: System prompt
settings.description: Override the default routing-classifier system prompt. Leave empty for the built-in strict-JSON instructions.

settings.name: model
settings.label: Model
settings.description: Leave as `auto` to use Smart Router (recommended). Explicit model names MUST exist in the platform provider/model registry.

settings.name: temperature
settings.label: Temperature
settings.description: Low values (0.0-0.2) give deterministic routing. Default 0.1.

ai.shortDescription: LLM picks one of N named routes based on the input. Drives downstream edge gating.
ai.whenToUse: When `condition` (JS expression) isn't expressive enough — e.g. 'route by severity tone', 'pick the channel that matches customer urgency', 'classify intent into one of these buckets'.
ai.examplePrompt: Route critical incidents to pagerduty, internal-team notifications to slack, customer comms to email.
ai.promptHints: Use descriptive route names (slack/email/pagerduty, not branch1/branch2/branch3). Always configure `fallbackRoute` for production flows — it's the difference between 'flow fails on LLM hallucination' and 'flow degrades to safe default'.

outputAssertions.name: route_picked
outputAssertions.errorMessage: llm_router did not produce a route selection.
